title: Reading: A Hindi Introduction
description: Read a simple self-introduction in Hindi and practice your comprehension.

blocks.passage:
📖 नमस्ते! (Namaste!)

नमस्ते! मेरा नाम जॉन है। (Namaste! Mera naam John hai.) - Hello! My name is John.
मैं अमेरिका से हूँ। (Main America se hoon.) - I am from America.
मैं एक छात्र हूँ। (Main ek chhatra hoon.) - I am a student.

मैं हिंदी सीखता हूँ। (Main Hindi seekhta hoon.) - I learn Hindi.
मुझे भारत पसंद है। (Mujhe Bharat pasand hai.) - I like India.

आपसे मिलकर खुशी हुई। (Aapse milkar khushi hui.) - Nice to meet you.
धन्यवाद! (Dhanyavaad!) - Thank you!

items.explanation: No, he is a student (छात्र - chhatra).
Yes, 'मुझे भारत पसंद है' (Mujhe Bharat pasand hai) means 'I like India'.

blocks.title: Introducing Yourself in India
blocks.content: When introducing yourself in India, it's polite to use 'Aap' (आप) for 'you' instead of 'Tum' or 'Tu'. 'Aap' shows respect, especially to elders or strangers. Also, people often ask 'आप कहाँ से हैं?' (Where are you from?) early in the conversation to show interest in your background.
